text.title: product-lifecycle Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for product-lifecycle. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Require at least 6 quarters of data before making a stage assessment. One-quarter dip does not equal Decline.
rule.rationale: Short data windows produce false stage calls. A temporary dip from seasonality or a one-off event looks identical to structural decline with fewer than 6 data points.

rule.statement: Use category-specific baselines for growth thresholds, not the classic defaults (20%=growth, 10%=maturity). Consumer apps: 100%+; B2B vertical SaaS: 30%; mature enterprise: 10%.
rule.rationale: LLMs anchor on framework defaults. Injecting category baselines prevents misclassifying a healthy 15%-growth B2B product as "Maturity" when its peers grow at 12%.

rule.statement: Sunset is a feature, not a failure: provide a clear migration path and grace period. Plan customer communications before announcing.
rule.rationale: Silent EOL destroys trust and forces customers to scramble. Proactive migration support preserves the relationship and reputation.

rule.statement: Strategy must match stage. Growth tactics on a declining product and efficiency focus on an early-stage product both waste resources.
rule.rationale: Mismatched strategy is the core failure lifecycle management solves. Growth spend on decline accelerates burn; efficiency focus on Introduction starves learning loops.

rule.statement: Every stage assessment must include an alt hypothesis: "what would I expect if this assessment is wrong?"
rule.rationale: Single-hypothesis stage calls are overconfident. An alt hypothesis makes the assessment falsifiable and triggers re-evaluation when signals diverge.

rule.statement: Start transition planning one quarter before the expected stage change. Capability gaps (team mix, infra, KPIs) take a quarter to fill.
rule.rationale: Stage transitions that catch teams unprepared result in wrong-stage activity continuing for months past the transition point, wasting resources.

rule.statement: Stage transition decisions (kill, sunset, double-down) are leadership decisions. Agent output is decision-support only; it never auto-publishes.
rule.rationale: Stage transition triggers resource reallocation, team changes, and customer communications. These have organizational authority requirements agents do not satisfy.
